# Vendor Parity Exemptions
# Commands that are intentionally NOT ported across all vendor adapters.
# Format: <vendor>:<command_name>  (one per line; # for comments)
# Vendors: claude | gemini | codex
#
# An "exemption" means: this command exists for vendor X but not vendor Y,
# and that's a deliberate choice — not a gap. Usually due to:
#   - Vendor-specific MCP (e.g. Figma MCP not available on Gemini CLI)
#   - Naming drift from a predecessor (war-room vs frontend-war-room)
#   - Feature-flagged rollout (Claude-first, Gemini catches up later)
#
# The file is read by scripts/validate-vendor-parity.sh.
# Declared in v2.1.4 (W4.16) — pre-existing gaps marked, NEW gaps still fail CI.

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Naming-drift exemptions — same capability, different name across vendors.
# TODO v2.2: rename Gemini commands to match Claude names (or vice versa).
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# `frontend-war-room` (Claude) vs `war-room` (Gemini) — same command,
# name-drift from early adapter implementation. Normalize in v2.2.
gemini:frontend-war-room
claude:war-room

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Deliberate vendor-specific commands
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# `market-scan` is Gemini-only — early experiment; Claude uses market-researcher agent instead.
# Evaluate in v2.2: promote to Claude as a wrapper around the agent, or drop from Gemini.
claude:market-scan

# `triage-build` (Claude, v2.1.3) — not yet ported to Gemini. Scheduled for v2.2 vendor-parity pass.
gemini:triage-build

# `ulak-scaffold` (Claude, v2.2.2) — scaffolder requires Claude Code's Write + subagent
# dispatch semantics with superpowers:saas-scaffolder skill. Gemini CLI adapter deferred to
# post-v1.0.0 (requires skill-equivalent abstraction in the Gemini runtime).
gemini:ulak-scaffold

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# v1.0.0-cycle community-ecosystem wrappers (added post-internal-v2.1 batch).
# These 6 commands wrap superpowers disciplines (TDD, brainstorming,
# subagent-driven-development, fourteen-dimension-audit, MCP governance,
# pattern extraction). Gemini CLI adapter is deferred to v1.1 — the underlying
# skills are Claude-Code-specific runtime constructs.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
gemini:ulak-audit-deep
gemini:ulak-brainstorm
gemini:ulak-mcp-discover
gemini:ulak-pattern-extract
gemini:ulak-subagent-dispatch
gemini:ulak-test-driven

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# v1.1.0-cycle vision-fix slash commands (Claude-Code-specific by design).
# These 6 commands realize the "don't search internet / don't write prompts /
# talk naturally / see what it is" vision layer. They wrap existing Claude
# Code primitives (slash-command dispatch, interactive prompts, file writes).
# Gemini CLI adapter is deferred to v1.2 pending equivalent primitives.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
gemini:ulak-ask
gemini:ulak-hello
gemini:ulak-locale
gemini:ulak-packs
gemini:ulak-search
gemini:ulak-start

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# v1.3.0-cycle post-scaffold onboarding command (Claude-Code-specific).
# `/ulak-next-steps` auto-chains after `/ulak-scaffold` to produce an 8-10
# step personalized runbook on screen (no disk writes). Relies on Claude
# Code's in-session chain dispatch after a successful scaffold. Gemini CLI
# adapter scheduled for v1.3 once equivalent primitive exists.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
gemini:ulak-next-steps

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# v3.0.x beginner-expansion slash commands (Claude-Code-specific).
# `/ulak-explain` — 5-field schema term explainer; lookup source is
#   `docs/runtime/beginner-glossary.md`, with Grep fallback across
#   `docs/catalog.md` + `docs/runtime/*.md` + `docs/governance/*.md`.
# `/ulak-demo`    — 3-project tour with measured file counts
#   (`find templates/... | wc -l`) and dev-day comparison. Relies on Claude
#   Code's Read+Grep+Bash(find) primitives. Gemini adapter post-v1.1.
# Both commands are inline-only (no disk writes) and join `/ulak-ask`,
# `/ulak-hello`, `/ulak-search` as the "talk naturally" surface.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
gemini:ulak-explain
gemini:ulak-demo
